params.name: Akebono
params.description:
On Oct 15, 1986 AD, Akebono was unearthed in Kamegaoka Heritage in Japan.
Akebono, a clay figure unearthed in Yamato, dates back to 4,000 years ago. It has a swollen belly and butt, a chubby body with an eddy-patterned round hat on the head. With long eyes, a pointy nose, and a small mouth, it has a pleasant look on its face. The clay contains mica which makes the figure shiny. The symbolic relic has been appointed as a national treasure of Japan.
